signals.title: USD — Fed Vigilance + Iran-US MOU + CESI
signals.text: St. Louis Fed's Musalem and NY Fed's Williams signal restrictive policy persistence, citing upward inflation pressures and productivity-driven real rate floors. This hawkish rhetoric coincides with a tentative 60-day US-Iran MOU, potentially easing energy-driven inflation tail risks. USD CESI at +19 (strong positive) confirms data outperformance supports the Fed's 'vigilant' stance. DXY at 98.98 remains supported by a +1.22% real rate, the highest in the G8.
evidence: Fed Musalem: real rate below long-run neutral

signals.title: NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 114.077 reflects a dominant G8 bilateral theme with a 1.154% spread between the week's leader and laggard. Despite NZD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading the bird), the RBNZ hike bias and NZD 1W outperformance (+0.775%) create acute short-squeeze risk. JPY remains the G8 laggard (-0.379%) as the -0.69% real rate maintains its status as the primary funding currency despite positive domestic data surprises (CESI +11).

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7168 shows a significant institutional vs retail divergence. COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) vs retail 72% short indicates retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend. Elevated vol premium (IV 13.7% vs HV30 8.0% = +5.7% VRP) suggests the market is paying heavily for protection against a trend break. The -72bp OIS carry advantage for AUD supports the long bias, but extreme retail shorting warns of a potential 'washout' before further upside.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.198 continues to hold below the 160.00 psychological and intervention level. While the +290bp SOFR-TONA carry spread favors USD, the options market is pricing protection against a JPY rally, with 25d RR at -1.11 (moderate put skew). JPY COT LF net short -81,624 remains crowded; any BoJ hawkish shift at the 16 June meeting risks a rapid unwind of carry positions. VIX at 15.8 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant for now.
